full_prompt: Design a monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum hull stringer / PV mast step for a collegiate solar-electric racing boat benchmark, inside the hull envelope limit of 6.0 m length x 2.4 m beam x 1.5 m above waterline height, using the benchmark 36 V sealed lead-acid propulsion battery and the prescribed PV mount rules with a lanyard tether. The part is a single extruded and machined 6061-T6 body (no welds, no bolted gussets, no bonded composite overlays) that spans 1200 mm fore-aft along the keelson, bonded via structural methacrylate (MMA) to fiberglass hull inner skin (the adhesive joint is outside this CAD prompt; the part itself is monolithic). The stringer cross section is a top-hat 80 mm wide at the flange, 60 mm tall web, 40 mm wide top, wall thickness 5 mm. Integral along the top are two M10 clearance holes (11 mm diameter) on 600 mm spacing for a removable PV mast base plate, and two pairs of M8 holes (9 mm) at 150 mm spacing at each end for rod-end link attachments to a forward thwart. Overall bounding envelope 1200 mm x 80 mm x 60 mm. Material 6061-T6 with E 68.9 GPa, nu 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa; marine fatigue knockdown factor 0.85 per ABYC H-8.3 for saltwater-immersed aluminum gives derated endurance limit ~70 MPa at 10^7 cycles. Operating environment: saltwater splash at 10-30 C, UV exposure from PV array. Three load cases. Load Case 1 (PV mast side load): a 1.6 kN lateral force applied to the aft M10 PV mount hole simulating 15 m/s beam wind on the 2.0 m^2 PV array mast arm (drag + moment resolved to the base), with the stringer bolted to the keelson fully fixed along its length (simulating composite bond). Load Case 2 (slam): a 3.5g vertical downward acceleration distributed on the stringer and the PV mount, equivalent to a 2.5 kN concentrated load at the aft M10 hole, representing a typical slam of a planing solar boat at 25 mph in 0.3 m chop. Load Case 3 (crash-stop / forward tow): a 2.5 kN horizontal aft-to-fore force at one of the rod-end M8 holes, simulating towing recovery after e-stop. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 (beam wind), peak von Mises must not exceed 138 MPa (yield / 2.0 per ABYC H-8 allowable for primary aluminum hull structure); (R2) under LC2 slam (short transient), peak stress must not exceed 184 MPa (yield / 1.5); (R3) under LC3 crash-stop, peak stress must not exceed 184 MPa; (R4) fatigue: assuming LC1 represents 10^7 cycles of wave-induced oscillation with a stress range equal to 30% of LC1 peak, the local fatigue stress range at any hole must remain below 70 MPa; (R5) a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis under LC2 (compressive mast loading into stringer) must yield a first-mode factor of at least 3.0; (R6) mass of the stringer shall not exceed 3.2 kg; (R7) first bending natural frequency (with mast base mass 4 kg lumped at the PV mount) must be above 25 Hz to avoid resonance with typical electric-outboard 1-3000 rpm rotating harmonics. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum hull stringer / PV mast step for a collegiate solar-electric racing boat, spanning 1200 mm fore-aft along the keelson and serving as the bonded mounting base for a removable PV mast and forward rod-end links. The part is a single extruded-and-machined top-hat section, monolithic (no welds, no bolted gussets). Design per ABYC H-8 marine aluminum allowables with the typical yield/2.0 (continuous) and yield/1.5 (transient) safety factors.

Envelope: overall bounding box on the order of 1200 x 80 x 60 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 PV-mast beam wind: 1.6 kN lateral at the aft PV mount hole (15 m/s beam wind on a 2.0 m^2 PV array), stringer fixed along the bonded length.
- LC2 slam: 2.5 kN vertical downward at the aft PV mount (3.5 g planing slam).
- LC3 crash-stop / forward tow: 2.5 kN horizontal fore-aft at one forward rod-end hole.
Also check a 10^7-cycle fatigue spectrum derived from LC1 and a modal case with a 4 kg mast base lumped at the PV mount.

prompt.title: 6061-T6 hull stringer / PV mast step for collegiate solar-electric boat benchmark
prompt.brief: Top-hat aluminum stringer with integral PV mast mount and rod-end attachments; survive beam-wind, slam, and crash-stop loads.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Wind loads reduced to equivalent static forces on mast.
